title: You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs
summary: Balázs Horváth argues that AI code generation has shifted the bottleneck from implementation to stakeholder management and requirements elicitation. He presents a framework using story mapping and value assessment to distinguish production agents from demos, grounded in a VisualLabs hackathon where 17 of 21 agent ideas were abandoned due to poor problem framing rather than technical limitations.
writeup: Balázs Horváth of VisualLabs argues that AI code generation has moved the bottleneck from implementation to stakeholder management and requirements elicitation. The talk presents a framework using story mapping and value assessment to separate production agents from demos. The grounding case is a VisualLabs hackathon where 17 of 21 agent ideas were abandoned, attributed to poor problem framing rather than technical limits. The takeaway: as code generation becomes cheap, the durable skill is eliciting requirements and framing the problem with stakeholders.
